document.title: AspenView-Security-Threat-Modeling
authorityOrJurisdiction: Medical Device Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC and annex Ⅱ, Ⅸ, X

sourceSummary.oneLine: Threat modeling document for AspenView software cybersecurity risks from external sources.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Identifies and analyzes potential cybersecurity risks for the AspenView system throughout its lifecycle and points to mitigation details in the Cybersecurity Risk Management Report.
keyPoints: AspenView interfaces with an X-ray detector through on-board Wi-Fi to display diagnostic images.
STRIDE is selected to identify spoofing, tampering, repudiation, information disclosure, denial of service, and elevation of privilege threats.
Lifecycle risk areas include supply chain, manufacturing and deployment, maintenance and update, and decommissioning risks.

Mitigations include supplier audits, secure SDLC, cryptographic signatures, secure configuration, encrypted update channels, role-based access controls, monitoring maintenance sessions, and decommissioning procedures.

entities.name: AspenView Software
entities.evidence: AspenView Software: Application used by healthcare providers.

entities.name: X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: X-ray Detector: Captures images and communicates with AspenView.

entities.name: Wi-Fi Module
entities.evidence: Wi-Fi Module: Facilitates wireless data transfer between the detector and software.

entities.name: STRIDE
entities.evidence: The STRIDE methodology is selected for its comprehensiveness in identifying Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege

entities.name: Medical Device Directive 93/42/EEC
entities.evidence: It meets the requirements of the Medical Device Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC and annex Ⅱ, Ⅸ, X.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity threat modeling
concepts.evidence: This cybersecurity document is to model the potential threat against the AspenView software from external sources.

concepts.name: STRIDE methodology
concepts.evidence: The STRIDE methodology is selected for its comprehensiveness in identifying Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk assessment
concepts.evidence: This document aims to identify and analyze potential cybersecurity risks associated with the AspenView system throughout its lifecycle, providing pre- and post-mitigation risk assessments.

concepts.name: Secure software development lifecycle
concepts.evidence: Implement a secure software development lifecycle (SDLC) that includes vetting of third-party components.

concepts.name: Update integrity
concepts.evidence: Updates might be intercepted or replaced with malicious versions if not properly secured.

concepts.name: Device decommissioning
concepts.evidence: Establish and follow strict decommissioning procedures, including data deletion and hardware disposal.

evidence.claim: The document date and revision are 2024.04.01 and Rev 0.
The modeled product is AspenView software.
AspenView is described as radiological image processing system technical documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: This Technical File consists of technical documentation relating to development of Radiological Image Processing System(AspenView).

evidence.claim: The document cites compliance with Medical Device Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC and annexes II, IX, and X.
evidence.evidenceText: It meets the requirements of the Medical Device Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC and annex Ⅱ, Ⅸ, X.

evidence.claim: AspenView interfaces with an X-ray detector through on-board Wi-Fi for diagnostic image display.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView is a medical software application that interfaces with an X-ray detector through on-board Wi-Fi to display images for diagnostic purposes.

evidence.claim: The document aims to identify and analyze cybersecurity risks throughout the AspenView lifecycle.
evidence.evidenceText: This document aims to identify and analyze potential cybersecurity risks associated with the AspenView system throughout its lifecycle, providing pre- and post-mitigation risk assessments.

evidence.claim: The system components listed are AspenView Software, X-ray Detector, and Wi-Fi Module.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView Software: Application used by healthcare providers. X-ray Detector: Captures images and communicates with AspenView. Wi-Fi Module: Facilitates wireless data transfer between the detector and software.

evidence.claim: Hospital networks are assumed to be hostile environments where adversaries may control network traffic.
evidence.evidenceText: Assume that hospital networks are hostile environments where adversaries may control network traffic, enabling them to alter, drop, and replay packets.

evidence.claim: STRIDE is selected as the threat methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: The STRIDE methodology is selected for its comprehensiveness in identifying Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege

evidence.claim: Identified cybersecurity risk areas include supply chain, manufacturing and deployment, maintenance and update, and decommission risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Supply Chain Risks; Manufacturing and Deployment Risks; Maintenance and Update Risks; Decommission Risks

evidence.claim: Comprehensive implemented mitigation measures are referenced in the AspenView Cybersecurity Risk Management Report section 7.
evidence.evidenceText: Please refer to AspenView Cybersecurity Risk Management Report section 7. Cyber Security Risk Mitigation for the comprehensive, mitigating measures that have been implemented against the identified risks in 2.5
